description: Verify that dashboard provide a primary operations view for live strategy, account health, Reservoir ranking, system health, and critical alerts.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-1 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can inspect live strategy status, IB account equity, buying power, margin, Reservoir rankings, heartbeat state, and active critical alerts without SSH access.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-UI-001, SRS-UI-002, SRS-UI-003 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that dashboard provide a strategy management view.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-2 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can view active strategies, deployed code version, mode, asset class, container status, and key metrics; live designation requires explicit confirmation.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-ORCH-004, SRS-EXE-001 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that dashboard provide backtest controls and result history.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-3 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can initiate backtests with strategy, date range, parameter overrides, and cost model configuration; user can inspect completed backtest details.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-BT-001, SRS-UI-004 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that dashboard provide a kill-switch control with confirmation and status feedback.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-4 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can activate kill switch and see cancellation, liquidation submission, timeout, notification, and disconnect status.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-SAFE-001, SRS-SAFE-002 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that dashboard provide Hot-Swap controls and status.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-5 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can trigger manual promotion, inspect demotion-pending state, view cool-down expiry, and see automatic-trigger configuration.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-RESV-003 through SRS-RESV-006 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that dashboard provide embedded Jupyter navigation.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and open the dashboard in a browser automation session.
Step 2: Navigate through the dashboard workflow for UI-6 and perform the user action or inspection described by the requirement.
Step 3: Verify acceptance criteria: User can access Jupyter from the dashboard workflow without direct service URL navigation.
Step 4: Confirm the UI behavior traces to SRS-RES-001, SRS-RES-003 and leave passes false until browser evidence is captured.

description: Verify that Python Strategy API exposes Scheduling, subscriptions, historical data, order submission, cancellation, callbacks, indicators, warm-up, logging, state access, and author documentation.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise Python Strategy API with Strategy container identity.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Scheduling, subscriptions, historical data, order submission, cancellation, callbacks, indicators, warm-up, logging, state access, and author documentation
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-SDK-001 through SRS-SDK-009 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that REST API exposes Strategy lifecycle, live designation, kill switch, Hot-Swap, backtest launch, backtest query, Reservoir ranking, watchlist configuration, system status, logs, alerts.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise REST API with Local single-user deployment controls.

Step 3: Verify required capability: Strategy lifecycle, live designation, kill switch, Hot-Swap, backtest launch, backtest query, Reservoir ranking, watchlist configuration, system status, logs, alerts
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-API-001 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that WebSocket API exposes Live dashboard updates for P&L, metrics, account status, heartbeat, logs, alerts, Reservoir ranking, and strategy state.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise WebSocket API with Local single-user deployment controls.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Live dashboard updates for P&L, metrics, account status, heartbeat, logs, alerts, Reservoir ranking, and strategy state
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-UI-001 through SRS-UI-004 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that CLI exposes Kill switch, strategy management, live designation, Hot-Swap, readiness status, and basic administration.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise CLI with Local shell access.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Kill switch, strategy management, live designation, Hot-Swap, readiness status, and basic administration
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-API-001, SRS-SAFE-001 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that Brokerage adapter interface exposes Order submission, cancellation, account status, positions, market data, historical data, and versioned adapter capability discovery.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise Brokerage adapter interface with Internal service boundary.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Order submission, cancellation, account status, positions, market data, historical data, and versioned adapter capability discovery
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-EXE-006, SRS-EXE-007 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that Data provider interface exposes Bulk equity download, historical backfill, incremental update, fundamentals ingestion, options import, and user Parquet import.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise Data provider interface with Internal service boundary.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Bulk equity download, historical backfill, incremental update, fundamentals ingestion, options import, and user Parquet import
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-DATA-001 through SRS-DATA-007 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that Unified historical data interface exposes Query by symbol, date range, resolution, asset class, normalization mode, and data source-neutral result shape.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and confirm the API, CLI, or strategy runtime surface is reachable.
Step 2: Use bash, CLI commands, HTTP/WebSocket requests, or a Python strategy fixture to exercise Unified historical data interface with Strategy, backtest, factor, and research consumers.
Step 3: Verify required capability: Query by symbol, date range, resolution, asset class, normalization mode, and data source-neutral result shape
Step 4: Confirm the interface contract traces to SRS-DATA-007, SRS-DATA-012 and leave passes false until contract evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Order submitted by a non-live strategy to live execution path with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-1: Order submitted by a non-live strategy to live execution path
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Reject synchronously with structured error and no IB order side effect.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-EXE-001, SRS-ERR-001 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles IB Gateway unreachable with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-2: IB Gateway unreachable
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Reject live order submission with CONNECTIVITY_BLOCKED, log event, alert dashboard, and attempt reconnection.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-SAFE-003, SRS-MD-005 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Market data stale with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-3: Market data stale
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Reject live and paper order submissions with MARKET_DATA_STALE until fresh data arrives.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-MD-004 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Market-data line limit exceeded with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-4: Market-data line limit exceeded
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Reject subscription with SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_REACHED and alert operator.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-MD-002 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Ingestion record validation failure with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-5: Ingestion record validation failure
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Quarantine invalid record, omit it from primary storage, and alert with reason counts.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-DATA-013 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles IB pacing budget exceeded by configured ingestion job with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-6: IB pacing budget exceeded by configured ingestion job
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Refuse to start affected job and alert operator at scheduling time.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-DATA-002, SRS-DATA-004 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Hot-Swap demotion liquidation timeout with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-7: Hot-Swap demotion liquidation timeout
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Enter demotion-pending state, notify operator, cancel unfilled order, and block promotion.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-RESV-004 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Kill-switch liquidation timeout with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-8: Kill-switch liquidation timeout
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Log unfilled order details, notify by email and SMS, cancel unfilled liquidation order, and disconnect from IB.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-SAFE-002 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.

description: Verify that the system handles Missing or invalid startup configuration with the required error behavior.
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh and configure the fixture or fault state that triggers the error condition.
Step 2: Trigger error condition ERR-9: Missing or invalid startup configuration
Step 3: Verify expected behavior: Hold system in pre-trade state and expose readiness failure through logs, dashboard, and API.
Step 4: Confirm logs, API responses, dashboard alerts, or side-effect absence trace to SRS-ARCH-005, SRS-MD-006 and leave passes false until evidence is captured.
